tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows evidence of renal dysfunction (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), hypoglycemia, metabolic acidosis (low CO₂), and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, bilirubinuria, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria). Hematocrit is slightly low. HbA1c is low, suggesting chronic hypoglycemia. No evidence of infection in urine. Findings are consistent with possible acute or chronic kidney disease with metabolic derangements; urgent clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
